Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that filters out cases from an event log table Cases are filtered out if they contain a specific 3 activity sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in direct succession AND the time difference between the 2nd and 3rd activities in that sequence is more than 5 days The final output should be all events from cases that do not match these criteria

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Key Requirements Table event_log with columns case_id activity timestamp Sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order direct succession no other events in between for the same case Timing condition timestamp of Confirm Order minus timestamp of Create Order more than 5 days Output All events from cases that do not satisfy both the sequence and timing conditions Database DuckDB supports window functions CTEs standard SQL features

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct the Problem Step 1 Identify events that form the specific sequence in direct succession Step 2 Check the timing condition for those sequences Step 3 Collect the case ids that match both conditions Step 4 Filter out events belonging to those case ids from the original table

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: How to find direct succession Use window functions LAG or LEAD to look at the previous and next activity within the same case ordered by timestamp

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Actually it is easier to flag events that are the middle of the sequence Create Order check if prev activity equals Approve Purchase Requisition and next activity equals Confirm Order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then I need the timestamps of Create Order and Confirm Order to check the 5 day condition I can get the next timestamp using LEAD timestamp over the same partition Then if I flag the Create Order event as the middle of the sequence I can compute next timestamp minus timestamp greater than interval 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets verify the sequence condition direct succession means exactly these three in a row The LAG and LEAD approach correctly checks immediate predecessor and successor The timing condition next timestamp minus timestamp greater than interval 5 days is valid

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait what if there are multiple events with the same timestamp ORDER BY timestamp might be non deterministic It is safer to use row numbering and then join or use LAG and LEAD on that But for simplicity in SQL benchmarks ORDER BY timestamp is usually acceptable I will stick with ORDER BY timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now I have matching cases with case ids to exclude Final query selects from event_log where case_id not in matching cases ordered by case_id and timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Potential issues NOT IN with NULLs If cases to exclude has NULLs NOT IN behaves unexpectedly But case_id should not be NULL I will use NOT EXISTS to be safer

Name: Validation - PE
Text: What about cases where the sequence appears but the time difference is less than or equal to 5 days They should not be excluded My query correctly requires both conditions

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets test mentally Case A with the exact sequence and more than 5 days is excluded Case B with another activity in between is not matched Case C with the sequence but only 3 days is not excluded

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: What if a case has multiple sequences one with more than 5 days and one with less than or equal to 5 days If any sequence matches the criteria the case is excluded Using distinct case_id handles that

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check direct succession is covered by LAG and LEAD time elapsed condition is covered by next timestamp minus timestamp greater than interval 5 days exclude matching cases is covered by NOT EXISTS
